title: Keep the stack aligned and the frame correct
description: Maintain the ABI-required stack alignment at call boundaries and lay out the frame so locals, saved registers, and return address are correct.
body: Use when setting up a function's stack frame or making calls from assembly. Do not let the stack pointer drift out of the required alignment (16-byte at the point of a call on x86-64/AArch64) and do not miscount the bytes you push. Actions: account for the return address and pushed registers when computing alignment, reserve frame space in one aligned adjustment, and restore the stack pointer exactly on exit. Validation signals include rsp/sp being 16-byte aligned at every call site (checkable in a debugger) and SSE/ABI-sensitive callees not crashing. Risk boundary: a misaligned stack causes intermittent crashes in aligned-load instructions deep in a callee; treat alignment as mandatory and verify it at call sites with the debugger.
